things.name: Bascarsija Old Bazaar
things.description: Sarajevo's beating heart — a 15th-century Ottoman bazaar with coppersmith workshops and cevapi

things.name: Latin Bridge & Assassination Site
things.description: The spot where Archduke Franz Ferdinand was assassinated in 1914, triggering World War One

things.name: Gazi Husrev-beg Mosque
things.description: The largest and most important Ottoman mosque in the Balkans, built in 1531

things.name: Yellow Fortress (Zuta Tabija)
things.description: Hilltop Ottoman fortress with the most panoramic views of Sarajevo's minarets and mountains

things.name: Tunnel of Hope Museum
things.description: The 800-metre tunnel dug under the runway during the 1992-95 siege to supply besieged Sarajevo

things.name: Sarajevo City Hall (Vijecnica)
things.description: Magnificent 1896 Moorish Revival building — destroyed in 1992, painstakingly restored in 2014

things.name: War Childhood Museum
things.description: Award-winning museum of personal objects documenting childhood experiences during the siege

things.name: Cable Car to Mount Trebevic
things.description: Restored cable car to the 1984 Winter Olympics bobsled track with spectacular valley views

things.name: National Museum of Bosnia and Herzegovina
things.description: The country's oldest museum housing the precious Sarajevo Haggadah manuscript from 1350

things.name: Mostar Day Trip
things.description: UNESCO-listed Ottoman town with the famous Stari Most bridge and cliff-diving tradition, 130 km south
